corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: the promise of deep learning is to discover rich hierarchical models that represent probability distributions over the kinds of data encountered in artificial intelligence applications such as natural images audio waveforms containing speech and symbols in natural language corpora so far the most striking successes in deep learning have involved discriminative models usually those that map a high-dimensional rich sensory input to a class label these striking successes have primarily been based on the backpropagation and dropout algorithms using piecewise

context_window.previous: we propose a new framework for estimating generative models via an adversarial process in which we simultaneously train two models a generative model g that captures the data distribution and a discriminative model d that estimates the probability that a sample came from the training data rather than g the training procedure for g is to maximize the probability of d making a mistake this framework corresponds to a minimax two-player game in the space of arbitrary functions g and d a unique solution exists with g recovering the training data distribution and d equal to 1 2 everywhere in the case where g and d are defined by multilayer perceptrons the entire system can be trained with backpropagation there is no need for any markov chains or unrolled approximate inference networks during either training or generation of samples experiments demonstrate the potential of the framework through qualitative and quantitative evaluation of the generated samples body

We propose a new framework for estimating generative models via an adversarial process, in which we simultaneously train two models: a generative model G that captures the data distribution, and a discriminative model D that estimates the probability that a sample came from the training data rather than G.

We propose a new framework for estimating generative models via an adversarial process, in which we simultaneously train two models: a generative model G that captures the data distribution, and a discriminative model D that estimates the probability that a sample came from the training data rather than G.
Experiments demonstrate the potential of the framework through qualitative and quantitative evaluation of the generated samples.

1 In the proposed adversarial nets framework, the generative model is pitted against an adversary: a discriminative model that learns to determine whether a sample is from the model distribution or the data distribution.
This framework can yield specific training algorithms for many kinds of model and optimization algorithm.

Prominent recent work in this area includes the generative stochastic network (GSN) framework , which extends generalized denoising auto-encoders : both can be seen as defining a parameterized Markov chain, i.e., one learns the parameters of a machine that performs one step of a generative Markov chain.
Compared to GSNs, the adversarial nets framework does not require a Markov chain for sampling.
